title: Least-to-Most Prompting Enables Complex Reasoning in Large Language Models

defines.name: least-to-most prompting
defines.definition: A two-stage prompting strategy that first decomposes a complex problem into simpler subproblems and then solves them sequentially using earlier subproblem answers as context.

problem: Chain-of-thought prompting often fails to generalize from easy demonstration examples to harder problems that require stepwise decomposition.
